<function=python>

before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

# Find what changed - look at positions of key objects
print("=== BEFORE ===")
for n in before['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('p', 'w', 'W', 'G', 'M') and n['pixels'] <= 20:

print("\n=== AFTER ===")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('p', 'w', 'W', 'G', 'M') and n['pixels'] <= 20:
        print(f"  {n['color']} id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, pos={n['boundary'][0]}")

# Check for new nodes or changed positions
print(f"\nBefore nodes: {len(before['nodes'])}, After nodes: {len(after['nodes'])}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
